messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Say hello world in a Node app with a single endpoint
Clarifications:
None
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Say hello world in a Node app with a single endpoint",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "init-node-project",
      "title": "Initialize Node project",
      "description": "Create a new project directory and run npm init to generate package.json with default values.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "A project directory exists with a valid package.json file at the root."
    },
    {
      "id": "install-dependencies",
      "title": "Install required dependencies",
      "description": "Install a minimal HTTP framework (e.g., express) or verify core Node is sufficient; add dependency entries to package.json.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "init-node-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Dependencies are listed in package.json and present in node_modules (or no deps required if using core http)."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-app-file",
      "title": "Create application entry file",
      "description": "Add the main app file (e.g., index.js or app.js) that will start the server and export or run the app.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "init-node-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "A runnable file (index.js) exists with a basic server skeleton (imports and server start code)."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-hello-endpoint",
      "title": "Implement the Hello World endpoint",
      "description": "Add a single HTTP endpoint (e.g., GET /) that responds with the text 'Hello World' and status 200.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "create-app-file",
        "install-dependencies"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Sending an HTTP GET request to the configured endpoint returns status 200 and body 'Hello World'."
    },
    {
      "id": "add-start-script",
      "title": "Add npm start script",
      "description": "Add a start script to package.json that runs the application entry file using node (e.g., node index.js).",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "init-node-project",
        "create-app-file"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Running npm start launches the server without errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "test-endpoint",
      "title": "Test the endpoint locally",
      "description": "Start the app and use curl or a browser to request the endpoint, verifying the response and status code.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "implement-hello-endpoint",
        "add-start-script"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Automated or manual test confirms GET / returns 'Hello World' with HTTP 200 on the running server."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 6,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Top-down functional decomposition: create project scaffold, install dependencies, add application entry, implement endpoint logic, add start script, then verify via testing. Dependencies flow from project initialization to implementation to verification."
}
